title: Export features
description: This initial feature set for this will allow a user to export the journal for the current day to either HTML or Markdown, and the file will be made available for download.
close_reason: Implementation of SOAP export features is complete, including backend logic, email integration with retries, server endpoints, and frontend UI.

title: Sharing box should be centered
description: When clicking "Share", the modal box should be centered in the page rather than in the top-left corner of the window.

title: Better UX for sharing
description: The UX for sharing the journal feels rather dated. It also is not dynamic enough. There should not be drop down boxes with incompatible options like 'markdown and email'. Perhaps the decision for 'Email' or 'Download' should be larger boxes that highlight or otherwise change when clicked by the user. Then the other available options may change; for example, selecting 'Email' would not show 'Markdown' as a formatting option.

title: email author should be "My SOAP"
description: Email sent from the application should have a friendly author name like 'My SOAP' rather than 'no-reply' as it is now.

title: email sends full daily scripture
description: Sending an email results in the entire daily reading being sent rather than the verse(s) selected by the user. If a user has *not* selected any verses this is an appropriate action. However, if there are selections they should be used. This may require requesting only these verses from the 'esv' package.

title: Build Frontend Export UI and Interaction
description: Add Share button, Export Modal, CSS styles, and JavaScript logic to internal/server/web/ for triggering exports.
close_reason: Implementation of SOAP export features is complete, including backend logic, email integration with retries, server endpoints, and frontend UI.

title: Implement Server Export Endpoint and Security
description: Add /export handler in internal/server/server.go with data isolation checks, route registration, and integration tests.
close_reason: Implementation of SOAP export features is complete, including backend logic, email integration with retries, server endpoints, and frontend UI.

title: Integrate Export with Email System
description: Implement persistent email export delivery for multiple recipients. Includes creating a queued_emails table (one row per recipient), a background worker for retries with exponential backoff (max 5), and a specialized inline-CSS HTML template for email body delivery. Only HTML format will be supported for email sharing.
close_reason: Implementation of SOAP export features is complete, including backend logic, email integration with retries, server endpoints, and frontend UI.

title: Implement Backend Export Logic (HTML/Markdown)
description: Create internal/export package including Exporter interface, HTMLExporter (using html/template), MarkdownExporter, and comprehensive unit tests.

value: Persistent email export delivery uses a queued_emails table and a background worker for reliable multi-recipient delivery with exponential backoff.
The export system supports HTML and Markdown downloads, but only HTML for email sharing (to ensure formatting consistency).
